current_milestone: M4_Presentable_IN_FLIGHT (P9 — Rendering, Audio & Visual Grammar, its sole phase). M3 stays CLOSED behind it. P10 (content authoring) remains unlocked by M3 but still follows P9 in the DAG.
completed_since_last_snapshot: S9.1 (grammar schema + StylePack seam + render/ isolation scans) was COMPLETED and committed by the user directly as 76f00fe before this continuation began (see the prior handoff). This continuation added small backlog notes on top (REQ-084, REQ-174, dm-0087-0089) which were committed+pushed as 7477700, then ran S9.2 through S9.5 to completion, per the explicit user instruction 'finish P9 till 9.5'.

S9.2 COMPLETED (REQ-071 realization share): render/style/Raster2D.ts (the enumerated 2D-drawing device seam + createTraceRecorder, a pure trace recorder — no real canvas anywhere in render/ yet) and render/style/paper/{PaperRng,Geometry,Palette,PaperBlocks,PaperTiles,KindDraw,PaperStylePack}.ts — a direct TypeScript port of src/assets/paper-asset-library.html's core generators. render/style/StylePack.ts (S9.1) was amended with rasterize(request, device): void + a determinism probe in validatePack (dm-0090). Two planning-time corrections ledgered: dm-0091 (the reference kit's Optimization elements — movingPlatform/conveyor — were ALREADY achromatic; no recolor was actually needed, correcting dm-0084's inaccurate claim) and dm-0092 (identitySeed is deliberately ignored for entities/terrain/player/goal — they are grammar VOCABULARY per bible principle 8, not per-instance-unique decor; base geometry is seeded from kind alone, dm-0078). The goal's pennant was recolored from the reference's original terracotta to GOAL_ACCENT (teal) per dm-0085. PAPER_STYLE_PACK (the default instance) passes validatePack against DEFAULT_GRAMMAR cleanly. 12 structural tests (PaperStylePack.test.ts) — command-trace equality only, no pixel inspection, matching the project's fake-over-canvas culture.

S9.3 COMPLETED (REQ-070/071/162/150 shares): render/scene/Camera.ts (pure exponential smoothing toward the player, render-layer-only state, never in WorldState) and render/scene/{DrawList,SceneCompiler}.ts — the pure projection compileScene(current, previous, alpha, grammar, pack, camera, viewport, tileSizePx) -> DrawList. Interpolates entity/player positions by alpha; resolves every visible role through the grammar to exactly one category (player is the reserved null-category, always-critical special case); culls entities via the P3 SpatialPartition quadtree READ-ONLY (REQ-162's own 'reuse, not reimplementation' wording); terrain culls by tile-range clamp, the goal by direct AABB check. Per-entity overlay state (laser on/off, door open/closed, collapsingFloor intact/cracking, gravityZone normal/invert) is derived ONLY from real WorldState/LevelDefinition fields — dm-0093 explicitly notes pressurePlate/spring have no live 'currently pressed/launching' WorldState signal yet and render idle/default (a documented scope limit, not a silent gap — re-deriving Sensors/trigger logic in the render layer was rejected outright). dm-0094 notes culling queries the quadtree against CURRENT (pre-interpolation) positions, a documented simplification. 11 tests (Camera.test.ts + SceneCompiler.test.ts): interpolation at alpha in {0,0.5,1}, grammar resolution, critical-survives-culling, state derivation, goal AABB culling, JSON round-trip purity, WorldState-unchanged proof.

S9.4 COMPLETED (REQ-161/162/170 shares): render/gl/{Gl2Device,Atlas,Batcher,GlRenderer}.ts (the WebGL2 executor pipeline) + render/pool/{Pool,ParticlePool}.ts (REQ-161 pooling). Gl2Device is the enumerated WebGL2RenderingContext subset + createTraceGl2Device (pure in-memory recorder, no GPU). Atlas shelf-packs cached bitmaps into fixed-size pages (AtlasProfile.pageSizePx, data), uploading each distinct bitmap id EXACTLY ONCE via an injected producePixels thunk (real rasterization is render/platform/'s job, S9.8 — not yet built; headless tests use fakePixelData). Batcher groups a DrawList into one Batch per atlas page — many identical terrain tiles collapse into ONE instanced drawInstanced call, proven (batches.length < drawList.length). Pool is generic, fixed-capacity, generation-counted (FIFO free-queue reuse, stale-handle/double-release safe no-ops, exhaustion returns null rather than evicting); ParticlePool is its REQ-161 named instantiation. DrawItem (S9.3) was amended (dm-0095) to carry the originating VisualRequest alongside its BitmapHandle, so the atlas can rasterize a bitmap it has never seen. dm-0096 explicitly scopes S9.4's batching to instanced same-page grouping — a full pre-composed 'one background bitmap per level' merge (the bible §5 / original plan language) is NOT built and is documented as a legitimate future optimization, not silently dropped. 14 tests (GlExecutor.test.ts + Pool.test.ts).

S9.5 COMPLETED (REQ-150/016 shares): render/feel/{FeelProfile,SquashStretch,ImpulseEvents,DefeatMarker}.ts. FeelProfile is versioned render-tuning data (squash/stretch curve, burst counts/speed/lifetime) — zero literals in the logic modules. SquashStretch derives a velocity-driven scale, identity at rest, sign-independent, clamped. ImpulseEvents: jumpFired/landed are pure edge detectors over two WorldState snapshots (so a burst fires EXACTLY ONCE, never on every tick of a held state); spawnBurst acquires from the S9.4 ParticlePool using an injected seeded RngFn (never Math.random, never the sim's own Rng). DefeatMarker resolves dm-0089 (S9.1's open question, ledgered as dm-0097): the marker's screen time comes from the EXISTING fixed-step/render decoupling (the shell will render every real display frame against the same committed 'defeated' snapshot before the next tick reloads it) — no new hold mechanism, no Lifecycle.ts change; a genuine sim-side grace-tick stays available as a documented fallback if playtesting ever shows this reads as too brief. S9.5's own end-to-end integration test (proving REQ-150's anticipation-frame requirement through the REAL SceneCompiler+PaperStylePack pipeline, not just unit-level trust) CAUGHT A REAL BUG from S9.2: PaperStylePack's stateMatters() incorrectly excluded the player role from state-sensitive caching (grouped with goal, which genuinely IS state-invariant), silently collapsing idle/prejump/air onto ONE cached bitmap — the anticipation squat would never have actually rendered. Fixed (dm-0098); render/paper_style_pack's PKG entry amended. 15 tests (GameFeel.test.ts).

Test suite grew monotonically throughout: 595 (S9.1 close, prior session) -> 607 (S9.2) -> 618 (S9.3) -> 630 (S9.4) -> 640 (S9.5). tsc/build clean at every slice. PKG: 82 -> 84 (S9.1, prior) -> 86 (S9.2) -> 88 (S9.3) -> 90 (S9.4) -> 91 (S9.5) nodes; hash s9-a1c0f3 -> s9-b2d1a4 (S9.1, prior) -> s9-c3e2b5 (S9.2) -> s9-d4f1c6 (S9.3) -> s9-e5a0d7 (S9.4) -> s9-f6b2e8 (S9.5). Ledger: 86 (S9.1 close, prior) -> 89 (backlog notes REQ-084/174 + dm-0089) -> 92 (S9.2) -> 94 (S9.3) -> 96 (S9.4) -> 98 (S9.5) decisions. Every slice ran the full nine-stage SDLC loop (archives in meta/runs/S9.1-S9.5.*).

User-directed backlog additions (recorded before S9.2 started, exact-string edits to docs/requirements_backlog.md, diff-verified): REQ-084 (§9, P10-owned) — campaign difficulty distribution Easy 20% / Medium 35% / Hard 25% / Harder 15% / Very Hard 5%, calibration data for P10 content authoring, enforced via REQ-142 macro curriculum validation. Neither REQ has any code yet beyond what S9.1's plan already implies P9 will deliver at S9.8.

exact_execution_focus.pending_logical_transformation: Start S9.6 — WebAudio procedural signatures + cue derivation (REQ-071 audio share; REQ-170 WebAudio share). Implement render/audio/*.ts: an AudioDevice seam (minimal AudioContext subset, mirroring the Gl2Device/Raster2D enumerated-subset pattern), deriveAudioCues(previous, current) -> AudioCue[] (pure, transition-driven: jump fired, landed, defeat, goal, checkpoint, surface-class — reuse ImpulseEvents.jumpFired/landed from S9.5 rather than re-deriving), and a PatchCompiler turning each category's SynthPatch (already defined in PaperStylePack.audioPatch, S9.2) into a node-graph plan the executor schedules. A master gain seam must exposemuteForBreak()/resume() for the future Poki ad-break flow (S9.8). Tests per the execution plan's S9.6 deliverable: cue derivation exactly-once per transition; each category's patch compiles to the expected node-graph plan against a FakeAudioDevice; six patches structurally distinct (already true since S9.2, just needs a compile-plan-level proof); mute/resume gates scheduling; derivation is pure (same transitions -> same cues). Run `node tools/sdlc/stage.js start S9.6 --title "WebAudio procedural signatures + cue derivation" --reqs REQ-071,REQ-170` first.

THE AXIOM extends fully through every render/ subtree built so far: rendering NEVER feeds back into simulation (interpolationAlpha stays read-only, dm-0004/dm-0082); Clock.advance's realDeltaSeconds argument is the ONLY door real time has into the sim; CANONICAL_PIPELINE remains the only sanctioned engine assembly order; Math.random is forbidden everywhere including render/ (visual RNG is identity-seeded or explicitly-injected-and-seeded, e.g. ImpulseEvents.spawnBurst's RngFn parameter).
